mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate minimizes the opportunity cost of equity relative to cash and compresses discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 1% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals tightening labor markets and rising household income expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts generate a broad demand surge that outpaces near-term productive capacity, lifting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 390422 and HOUST at 629.0 in latest prints
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices alongside a near-zero federal funds rate produce deeply negative real interest rates that force capital reallocation from fixed income into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 224.304 and FEDFUNDS at 0.07 as of latest prints
failure_condition: In triggered periods, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative more than 45% of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals deteriorating corporate earnings momentum and rising capacity underutilization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a triggered INDPRO contraction over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects aggregate income expansion that drives consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between PAYEMS 3-month change and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP and retail sales indicates a demand expansion that is both broad and deep, generating outsized positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts independently.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to add incremental explanatory power with incremental R-squared below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding retail sales growth signals inventory accumulation and impending margin compression as supply outstrips final demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in INDPRO exceeds 3-month change in RSAFS
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the INDPRO 3-month change exceeds the RSAFS 3-month change over any 3-year rolling window.
